title: Habenaria peramoena

This classification places it within the order Asparagales and the subfamily Orchidoideae. Furthermore, it belongs to the genus Habenaria, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. intro: Habenaria peramoena, commonly known as the "Coralroot," is a type of orchid native to North America, characterized by its distinctive red and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the roots of Habenaria peramoena have been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, particularly peramoena, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Habenaria peramoena is often prepared in teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as eczema, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
